title: How single-party primary elections are reshaping Congress
description: Some lawmakers are speaking out against closed, single-party primaries, which they see as part of a system that limits voter choice and incentivizes elected officials to prioritize party loyalty.

title: What it means to be a man is a theme in Texas Senate race as Paxton attacks Talarico
description: Soon after winning the Texas Republican Senate primary runoff, Ken Paxton attacked Democratic nominee, state Rep. James Talarico as "too low-T for Texas," putting manhood front and center in the race.

title: Trump’s doctor says he is in ‘excellent health’ after latest checkup
description: While the president’s medical reports says he remains fit to serve, independent physicians have raised questions about the recurrent bruises on his hands and swelling in his legs.

title: Pentagon recruiting troops to watch White House UFC fights, memos show
description: Internal messages reviewed by The Post stipulate that military personnel must pay their own ways and meet strict physical requirements to be eligible.

title: How primaries are shaping Congress
description: The Primary used to be just the first step in the electoral process — now the primary has transformed, in many places into the moment when the election is all but decided. It's shaping Congress.

title: Trump's name must come off of the Kennedy Center, judge rules
description: The judge wrote in his 94-page ruling that it was "crystal clear" that the arts complex was named for the late president John F. Kennedy. He also ruled that the center could not wind down its programming and close for two years of renovations – at least for now

title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

title: What happened under Trump this week
description: He’s fighting hard to restrict mail voting.

title: Louisiana lawmakers pass a congressional map to dismantle a majority-Black district
description: Louisiana's Republican lawmakers raced to eliminate one of two majority-Black congressional seats in the state after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled the current map unconstitutional in a sweeping ruling.

title: A look at the race to become the next mayor of Los Angeles
description: In the upcoming primary for mayor of Los Angeles, incumbent Karen Bass is defending from two main challengers: a conservative reality TV star and a progressive city council member.

title: Replacing aging U.S. voting equipment will take years and billions of dollars
description: America's voting systems are getting old. But unless Congress makes a massive financial commitment, a new report finds it could take decades before voting machines are widely replaced.

title: Bondi doubles down on her handling of Epstein files in testimony to Congress
description: In a closed-door interview, Bondi said there were redaction errors, but otherwise defended the Justice Department's release of files in the Epstein case. She was ousted as attorney general in April.

title: Texas State Democratic Rep. James Talarico on race to flip Senate seat
description: NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Texas State Democratic Rep. James Talarico in the aftermath of the primary, which solidified Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton as his GOP competitor in November.

title: A look at the race to become the next mayor of Los Angeles
description: In the upcoming primary for Mayor of Los Angeles, incumbent Karen Bass is defending from two wildly different frontrunning challengers. Conservative reality TV star Spencer Pratt, and progressive city council member Nithya Raman.

title: Michigan’s Whitmer walks back comment saying she won’t run for president in 2028
description: Whitmer, term-limited after two gubernatorial wins in her battleground state, has been widely viewed as a potential Democratic candidate.

title: Vance says military should never let AI make life-and-death decisions
description: The vice president’s speech to Air Force Academy graduates comes as the Pentagon moves forward with using AI in war.

title: Supreme Court sides with Mississippi man on death row in racial bias case
description: For two decades, Terry Pitchford has agued his conviction was not valid, pointing to what he says were racially biased selections of the jurors who heard the case.

title: What are Republicans going to campaign on?
description: Trump is unpopular. Gas prices are high. Republicans are searching for a midterm message.

title: After stern words from Trump, U.S. and Iran trade strikes
description: The renewed violence came after the president said Iran was wrong if it thought he would feel pressure to make concessions and resolve the standoff before U.S. midterm elections later this year.

title: Trump’s approval plunges among his White working-class base
description: In a striking shift, White voters without college degrees who voted to reelect Trump by a huge margin are now net-negative on his job approval.
